display.purpose: Section H1 titles at the top of every editor page.

label.purpose: Breadcrumb eyebrows, field micro-labels, status pills.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Default action button — form submits, primary CTAs.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #1D1D1F; color: #FFFFFF; padding: 0 0.625rem; height: 2rem; border-radius: 0.5rem; border: none; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; transition: background 0.15s ease, transform 0.1s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: rgba(29,29,31,0.8); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(110,110,115,0.5); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { background: #FFFFFF; color: #1D1D1F; padding: 0 0.625rem; height: 2rem; border-radius: 0.5rem; border: 1px solid #D2D2D7; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; transition: background 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: #E8E8ED; } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(110,110,115,0.5); }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Read-only state pill, e.g. "Already in place".
components.css: .ds-badge-success { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: #F0FDF4; color: #15803D; border: 1px solid #BBF7D0; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 0.125rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.75rem; height: 1.25rem; }

components.name: Text Input
components.css: .ds-input { background: #FFFFFF; color: #1D1D1F; border: 1px solid #D2D2D7; border-radius: 0.625rem; padding: 0.25rem 0.625rem; height: 2rem; font-size: 0.875rem; transition: border-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #6E6E73; } .ds-input:hover { border-color: rgba(110,110,115,0.6); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: #6E6E73; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(110,110,115,0.5); }

components.name: Card
components.description: Standard content container — flat, bordered, no shadow.
components.html:
Card title
Card content goes here.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #FFFFFF; border: 1px solid #D2D2D7; border-radius: 0.875rem; padding: 1rem; } .ds-card-title { font-weight: 600; font-size: 1rem; color: #1D1D1F; margin-bottom: 0.25rem; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 0.875rem; color: #6E6E73; }

narrative.overview: ISSP Builder looks like a well-organized public servant's desk, not a corporate SaaS dashboard: plain, dependable, and built for someone getting real compliance work done under a deadline, not for someone being sold a product. The system defaults to the OS's own native font stack rather than a custom display face — the app is meant to disappear into the task the way a well-worn desk does, not announce itself as software. Density is generous but not cramped: forms breathe enough to read comfortably, but nothing is padded out for visual effect. The system explicitly rejects two things: it should never read as a sterile government Word-doc clone, and it should never read as a flashy consumer SaaS product.
keyCharacteristics: Flat by default; shadow is a functional signal reserved for floating overlays, never decoration.
One neutral, near-black ink color carries nearly all text and primary actions — color is spent on state and Part identity, not on flourish.
Native-feeling typography (system font stack) over a custom display face, reinforcing tool over product.
Every interactive control has an honest affordance: if it looks clickable, it acts clickable.

rules.name: The Spent-Not-Spread Rule
rules.body: Color is either a semantic state or a Part identity marker. It is never applied for visual variety alone.

rules.name: The One Face Rule
rules.body: Exactly one font family per theme mode carries display, body, and label together.

rules.name: The Floating-Only Rule
rules.body: Shadow is never applied to anything in normal page flow, no matter how important.

dos: Do keep color attached to meaning — semantic state or Part identity — never decoration.
Do keep every surface flat at rest; reserve shadow strictly for floating overlays.
Do give every checklist/toggle control an honest, minimal form.
Do use the system font stack for the default themes so the app feels native to the OS.
donts: Don't make this feel like a sterile government Word-doc clone.
Don't make this feel like a flashy consumer SaaS product.
Don't use a colored left-stripe border as a decorative accent on any card, list item, or callout.
Don't nest bordered card containers inside other bordered card containers.
Don't apply an arbitrary rainbow of colors to differentiate items already identified by their label text.
